title: Mirabilis limosa
taxonomy: Mirabilis limosa belongs to the family Nyctaginaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as four o'clock flowers or fuchsia family. The genus Mirabilis is native to the Americas and consists of several species, including M. limosa. Within the Nyctaginaceae family, Mirabilis limosa is a unique species that thrives in wet environments. The classification of Mirabilis limosa is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower color. As a member of the Nyctaginaceae family, Mirabilis limosa exhibits distinct adaptations to its environment.

side_effects: Mirabilis limosa, also known as the quaking grass, is generally considered a non-toxic and relatively safe plant species. intro: Mirabilis limosa, also known as Marsh Five-spot, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Four O'Clock family (Nyctaginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as wound infections and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Mirabilis limosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Mirabilis limosa is often prepared as a tincture or infused as a tea, and it is also used in topical creams and ointments for its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory properties.
